channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: It’s finally happening: the Steam Deck is officially coming to Australia
item.description: The Steam Deck is now being sold by Valve in Australia, with OLED and LCD models available to PC gamers on the go Down Under.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: Reports
item.description: "Services are currently stopped to upgrade internal systems," Kahle wrote in a Thursday update. "We are working to restore services as quickly and safely as possible. Sorry for this disruption.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: NYT Connections today — hints and answers for Friday, October 11 (game #488)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors from mines in a western North Carolina community harmed by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sibelco Reopens Operations After Hurricane Helene Disruption - A Boost to Local Economy
item.description:
Sibelco, a leading producer of high-purity quartz and silica sand, has resumed operations at its Spruce Pine, NC mining and processing facilities following the shutdown caused by Hurricane Helene. The company's restart is expected to contribute significantly to rebuilding the local economy.")

Sibelco Reopens Operations After Hurricane Helene Disruption - A Boost to Local Economy

The devastating effects of Hurricane Helene on the mountain town in western North Carolina have finally subsided, allowing Sibelco to resume operations at its Spruce Pine, NC mining and processing facilities. The company's announcement has been met with optimism as it is expected to contribute significantly to rebuilding the local economy.

Achieving Resilience Amidst Adversity

As Hurricane Helene swept through the region, Sibelco's operations were severely impacted. With power lines leading to their plants experiencing significant damage, the company faced an uphill battle in resuming production. However, a well-planned approach and efficient execution allowed them to overcome these challenges.

Main Point 1: A Statement of Intent

In a statement released by Sibelco CEO Hilmar Rode, he highlighted the importance of restarting operations and resuming shipments to customers in rebuilding the local economy. This declaration marked a significant step towards overcoming the adversity brought about by Hurricane Helene.

Achieving Operational Resilience

Sibelco's infrastructure sustained only minor damage, allowing them to repair power lines leading to their plants and restore production efficiently. The company worked closely with customers to assess their needs and plan the restart of product shipments as soon as possible. This proactive approach ensured a smooth transition from shutdown to operation.

Main Point 2: Addressing Capacity Shortfalls

With increasing demand for high-purity quartz, Sibelco had previously announced plans to double its output through a $200 million investment. The company's ability to resume operations at its Spruce Pine facility has come as a welcome relief in addressing capacity shortfalls in the industry.

The Impact of Supply Chain Disruptions

As highlighted by experts, disruptions to supply chains can have far-reaching consequences on industries reliant on high-purity quartz. Sibelco's operational resilience during this challenging period has helped mitigate these effects and maintain stability within the market.

Main Point 3: Ensuring Supply Chain Continuity

The Quartz Corp, another significant player in the industry, also faced disruptions due to Hurricane Helene. While their exact status remains uncertain, they have emphasized the importance of maintaining sizable safety stocks during these turbulent times.

Expert Assessments and Restoration Plans

A spokesperson for The Quartz Corp has announced plans to engage with experts to assess damage to their plants and develop restoration strategies accordingly. This approach ensures that any disruptions are minimized, and production is resumed in a controlled manner, thereby maintaining supply chain continuity.

Main Point 4: Industry Reactions

Industry observers have welcomed Sibelco's decision to resume operations as a significant boost to the local economy. While challenges persist, this proactive approach is expected to mitigate the impact of supply chain disruptions on industries reliant on high-purity quartz.

A Boost to Local Economy

The restarting of Sibelco's operations at its Spruce Pine facility has brought relief to a region that was severely affected by Hurricane Helene. This development serves as a testament to the resilience and determination of local businesses in the face of adversity.

Conclusion

In conclusion, Sibelco's decision to reopen its operations following the disruption caused by Hurricane Helene marks a significant step towards rebuilding the local economy. As the industry continues to navigate supply chain challenges, this proactive approach is expected to have far-reaching consequences for stakeholders across the market.

References

This article was compiled using information from various sources, including Sibelco's statement and expert assessments provided by The Quartz Corp. For more information on the impact of Hurricane Helene on local businesses and industry developments, please refer to relevant news articles and reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI sees continued attempts to use AI models for election interference
item.description: OpenAI has seen continued attempts by cybercriminals to use its artificial intelligence (AI) models for fake content aimed at interfering with this year’s elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Amazon’s new AI-powered spotlight tech aims to help delivery drivers find packages faster
item.description: Amazon, in its quest for greater efficiency, has developed new systems to shave seconds off each package delivery and to help customers make faster buying choices, even for new product types that they may know little about.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Google could be forced to sell parts of its business if the DOJ gets its way
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to force Google to sell parts of its business in order to eliminate its online search monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, The Hill

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Embracing your inner on-premises self
item.description: In a significant development in the cloud computing industry, AWS, a leading hyperscaler, has recently acknowledged considerable competition from on-premises IT solutions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Oracle touts ‘tip and tail’ release model for Java library development
item.description: Oracle is touting a “tip and tail” model for Java library development that the company says would give application developers a better experience and at the same time help library developers innovate faster.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 ways companies can use time series forecasting
item.description: Time series forecasting is a powerful machine learning method that leverages historical time-stamped data to predict future events and help reduce uncertainty from business conditions — for example, to accurately predict sales, inventory levels, and even manufacturing data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI-generated fake nudes now part of Russian cybercrime group's method
item.description: Cybercrime group Fin7 has created at least seven websites serving malware through generators that create fake nude images.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Fargo' creator: 'We've got a fight on our hands'
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation
item.description: Cybersecurity experts from the Army Software Factory, SSA and USCIS say letting developers drive security efforts drives up safety in code. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Google said Friday it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KREX 5
